9108dddf.title: Science Basics
9108dddf.desc: An interactive guided lesson on science fundamentals.

pages.title: What is the Solar System?
pages.html:
Our Solar System consists of the Sun and the objects that orbit it, including eight planets, dwarf planets, moons, asteroids, and comets.

The eight planets in order from the Sun are: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.

🌍 Earth is the third planet from the Sun and the only known planet to harbor life.

pages.qtext: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
pages.exp: Mars appears red due to iron oxide (rust) on its surface.

pages.title: Photosynthesis
pages.html:
Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide into glucose and oxygen.

The formula is:
6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂

This process happens in chloroplasts, the green organelles in plant cells.

pages.qtext: What gas do plants absorb during photosynthesis?
pages.exp: Plants absorb CO₂ and release oxygen as a byproduct.
The Sun is a star, not a planet. It is the center of our solar system.
